    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:claim/canonical-assembles-federated-stack-layer-substrates",
      "title": "The canonical substrate assembles federated stack-layer substrates",
      "type": "claim",
      "subtype": "implementation_claim",
      "importance": 5,
      "summary": "The canonical substrate is no longer only a monolithic description of XanaNode; it explicitly points to the real stack-layer substrate sources that make the system work.",
      "content": "The canonical substrate should teach that the stack is composed, not merely listed. Its project nodes describe the layers, and its federated substrate source nodes point at the real repositories and substrate roots for those layers. That lets people inspect the stack as a working federation of independently authored and versioned parts instead of assuming the canonical bundle is only a flat merge.",
      "relationships": []
    },
    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:claim/canonical-distinguishes-embedded-examples-from-federated-sources",
      "title": "The canonical substrate distinguishes embedded examples from federated sources",
      "type": "claim",
      "subtype": "clarification_claim",
      "importance": 5,
      "summary": "Example namespaces inside the canonical bundle are carried as embedded teaching records, while stack-layer substrate sources are represented as real federation targets.",
      "content": "The canonical bundle contains several kinds of composition at once. Example namespaces such as example.minimal, example.museum, example.researcher_a, and example.researcher_b are embedded inside the bundle for teaching, testing, and portable demonstration. By contrast, the Protocol, Core, Workspace, Studio, Mobile, and Hugo substrate entries point to real independently versioned substrate sources. That distinction matters because it tells readers what is bundled here and what exists as its own substrate outside the bundle.",
      "relationships": []
    },
    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:claim/copy-paste-breaks-lineage",
      "title": "Copy and paste breaks lineage",
      "type": "claim",
      "subtype": "problem_statement",
      "status": "asserted",
      "importance": 5,
      "summary": "When knowledge is reused by duplication instead of reference, provenance, attribution, surrounding context, and revision history become harder to preserve.",
      "content": "Ordinary reuse usually means making another anonymous copy. Once a quote, summary, screenshot, or excerpt is pasted into a new document, the connection back to the exact source version often becomes social rather than structural.\n\nXanaNode treats that break as a core failure mode. Addressable fragments, typed relationships, and transclusion let a later projection show not only the reused passage, but where it came from, which version it came from, and how it is being used now.",
      "relationships": []
    },
    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:claim/documents-collapse-knowledge-structure",
      "title": "Document boundaries collapse knowledge structure",
      "type": "claim",
      "subtype": "problem_statement",
      "status": "asserted",
      "importance": 5,
      "summary": "When a document is treated as the main unit of knowledge, many of the relationships that give meaning to the material become implicit, hidden, or lost.",
      "content": "A page, file, note, or database row can store information, but it does not automatically preserve why one idea supports another, where a quote came from, who disagreed, or what later changed. Those relationships usually live in a human reader's head or in conventions that a machine cannot reliably inspect.\n\nThat is why document-centric systems feel good for publication but weak for durable knowledge transfer. The information survives. The structure that made it useful often does not.",
      "relationships": []
    },
    {
      "relationships": [],
      "id": "xananode.canonical:claim/implementations-identify-compatibility",
      "title": "Implementations Identify XanaNode Compatibility",
      "type": "claim",
      "subtype": "normative",
      "status": "asserted",
      "importance": 5,
      "summary": "Implementations should identify themselves as XanaNode-compatible and reference the canonical specification without implying official ownership."
    },
    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:claim/local-first-without-federation-is-not-enough",
      "title": "Local-first without federation is not enough",
      "type": "claim",
      "subtype": "problem_statement",
      "status": "asserted",
      "importance": 5,
      "summary": "Owning local files is important, but durable knowledge transfer also requires a shared protocol for portability, identity, raw assets, and federation.",
      "content": "Local-first tools solve a real problem: people should own their data. But ownership alone does not create interoperability. If a tool stores files locally without a strong substrate model for node identity, relationships, fragments, assets, and exchange, the knowledge still tends to stay trapped inside that tool's habits and assumptions.\n\nXanaNode tries to keep the benefits of local-first authorship while adding a protocol layer that allows federation, mounting, merging, projection, and transport without discarding meaning.",
      "relationships": []
    },
    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:claim/projections-are-lenses-over-a-stable-substrate",
      "title": "Projections are lenses over a stable substrate",
      "type": "claim",
      "subtype": "definition",
      "status": "asserted",
      "importance": 5,
      "summary": "Studio, Mobile, Hugo, and future tools are projections of the same substrate rather than separate competing sources of truth.",
      "content": "A projection is the interface a person happens to use, not the canonical knowledge itself. The same substrate should be able to appear as a desktop workbench, a mobile capture tool, a static website, a printed booklet, or another compatible interface without rewriting the underlying node identities, relationships, fragments, or media.\n\nThis is why XanaNode separates the substrate from the projection layer. The lens can change. The knowledge should not have to start over.",
      "relationships": []
    },
    {
      "relationships": [],
      "id": "xananode.canonical:claim/protocol-docs-licensed-cc-by-4-0",
      "title": "Protocol Docs Are Licensed CC-BY 4.0",
      "type": "claim",
      "subtype": "definition",
      "status": "asserted",
      "importance": 5,
      "summary": "XanaNode protocol documentation is licensed under CC-BY 4.0 so the docs can be shared and adapted with attribution."
    },
    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:claim/relationships-preserve-knowledge",
      "title": "Relationships Preserve Knowledge",
      "type": "claim",
      "subtype": "definition",
      "status": "asserted",
      "importance": 5,
      "summary": "Knowledge survives movement between tools when relationships carry meaning, evidence, authorship, and addressable context instead of collapsing into anonymous links.",
      "content": "Relationships preserve knowledge because they carry the structure that explains why one thing matters to another. A claim is not equivalent to its source, a person is not equivalent to the work they authored, and a fragment is not equivalent to the whole document it came from.\n\nIf a system preserves only isolated notes or files, much of that meaning dissolves during copying, migration, summarization, or machine processing. When the typed relationships remain intact, the knowledge can still be inspected as a coherent structure with evidence, disagreement, provenance, lineage, and context.",
      "relationships": []
    },
    {

      "id": "xananode.canonical:claim/stack-demonstrates-federation-through-layer-substrates",
      "title": "The XanaNode stack demonstrates federation through layer substrates",
      "type": "claim",
      "subtype": "architectural_claim",

      "content": "Protocol, Core, Workspace, Studio, Mobile, and Hugo each publish or point to their own substrate source. The canonical substrate can therefore link to real substrate targets for the stack, not only to prose about the stack. This is a stronger proof than a diagram alone because each layer is addressable, versionable, and independently federatable.",

    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:claim/xananode-is-an-open-kernel-for-knowledge",
      "title": "XanaNode is an open kernel for knowledge",
      "type": "claim",
      "subtype": "comparative",
      "status": "asserted",
      "importance": 5,
      "summary": "XanaNode should function as a shared substrate layer for knowledge the way Linux functions as a shared kernel layer for computing: open, reusable, and separable from any one end-user interface.",
      "content": "The Linux comparison matters because it shifts XanaNode out of the category of one more application. The protocol, Core SDK, workspace behavior, fragment model, and federation rules are intended to become the shared plumbing underneath many projections rather than a single locked experience.\n\nThat is why the substrate must remain durable when a viewer changes. If the underlying knowledge survives the loss of one projection, the protocol is doing the job a kernel-like substrate layer is supposed to do.",
      "relationships": []
    },

      "type": "concept",

      "importance": 4,
      "summary": "The canonical substrate carries several teaching/example namespaces directly inside its own bundle without pretending they are all live mounted external substrates.",
      "content": "The canonical substrate includes preserved namespaced records from example.minimal, example.museum, example.researcher_a, and example.researcher_b. These examples are embedded as part of the canonical teaching bundle so readers and tools can inspect federation patterns, extension patterns, claims, sources, trails, and possible-match mappings in one portable substrate.     {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:concept/knowledge-substrate",
      "title": "Knowledge Substrate",
      "type": "concept",
      "subtype": "frame",
      "importance": 5,
      "summary": "The graph-shaped structure that lets information remain understandable across time by making claims, sources, media, fragments, and relationships explicit.",
      "content": "A knowledge substrate is not the same thing as a pile of information. It is the structure that makes information navigable, reviewable, and preservable over time.\n\nIn XanaNode, a substrate keeps nodes, relationships, raw assets, fragments, provenance, and authorship together so meaning does not disappear when the material moves between tools or formats. The same facts arranged through different relationships can produce very different explanatory power, which is why the structure itself matters.\n\nA substrate can be authored locally, transmitted as a portable bundle, mounted from another source, or projected into a site, app, archive, or physical medium. The substrate is the underlying knowledge; projections are only lenses over it.",
      "relationships": []
    },

    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:concept/protocol-artifacts",
      "title": "Protocol Artifacts",
      "type": "concept",
      "importance": 5,
      "summary": "The portable JSON files, schemas, fragments, reports, and media references that allow tools to validate, compare, mount, import, merge, and render substrates.",
      "content": "Protocol artifacts are the portable files that make a XanaNode substrate inspectable by both people and machines. They include the manifest, node records, relationship lists, fragments, validation reports, suggestions, registries, media references, and any other standardized outputs the protocol defines.\n\nThese artifacts matter because tools should not need private in-memory state or one specific application in order to understand a substrate. If the artifacts are present and valid, another compatible tool should be able to open the substrate, validate it, compare it, federate it, or project it.\n\nWhen the protocol stack is working properly, the artifacts are not a side effect. They are part of the knowledge being preserved and transmitted.",
      "relationships": []
    },
    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:concept/self-federating-stack",
      "title": "Self-Federating Stack",
      "type": "concept",
      "subtype": "architecture_pattern",
      "importance": 5,
      "summary": "The XanaNode stack demonstrates federation by treating its own major layers as independently versioned substrates that can still interoperate as one system.",
      "content": "XanaNode is not only a protocol with external federation examples. The stack itself is federated. Protocol, Core, Workspace, Studio, Mobile, Hugo, Canonical, Alphabet, and Lineage are all represented as distinct substrates or substrate targets with their own namespace, repository, transport shape, and governance path. The canonical bundle also carries embedded example namespaces for teaching and testing, but those are not the same thing as the real stack-layer substrate sources. Together, these two patterns show that each layer can stand on its own while the layers can still be mounted, compared, validated, projected, and carried together without collapsing into one monolithic blob.",
      "relationships": []
    },

    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:concept/trifecta-of-computing-failure",
      "title": "The Trifecta of Computing Failure",
      "type": "concept",
      "subtype": "problem_frame",
      "importance": 5,
      "summary": "A three-part failure pattern in modern computing where knowledge is trapped in isolated documents, copied without durable lineage, and kept in tools that do not naturally federate.",
      "content": "The Trifecta of Computing Failure names the pattern XanaNode is trying to escape.\n\nFirst, most systems treat the document or file as the primary container of meaning. Second, when people need to reuse or synthesize knowledge, they usually copy and paste excerpts into new places, which severs provenance, surrounding context, and revision lineage. Third, even tools that improve local note-linking rarely provide a durable protocol for independent authorship, portable raw assets, and federation across many tools and owners.\n\nXanaNode is meant to answer all three at once: relationships become first-class, transclusion and fragment identity preserve lineage, and the substrate can move between projection layers without collapsing back into one app's private format.",
      "relationships": []
    },
    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:concept/xananode",
      "title": "XanaNode",
      "type": "concept",
      "subtype": "protocol",
      "importance": 5,
      "summary": "A protocol for independently authored knowledge substrates that preserve relationships, provenance, lineage, disagreement, and addressable fragments so knowledge can move across tools and media without losing its structure.",
      "content": "XanaNode is a protocol for independently authored knowledge substrates that preserve relationships, provenance, lineage, disagreement, and addressable fragments, so knowledge can move across tools and media without losing its structure.\n\nThe protocol does not define a single viewer or one mandatory application. It defines the durable substrate shape, the validation rules, the registries, and the artifact contracts that let many tools read, compare, merge, and project the same knowledge.\n\nA XanaNode-compatible stack should be able to carry people, places, things, ideas, claims, questions, evidence, uncertainty, raw assets, and typed relationships in a form that survives transmission. That is why the protocol centers relationships, authorship, source lineage, and stable references instead of treating a document as the only meaningful unit.",
      "primary_media": "xananode.canonical:media/xananode-icon",
      "relationships": []
    },
    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:essay/what-is-xananode",
      "title": "What Is XanaNode?",
      "type": "essay",
      "subtype": "explainer",
      "importance": 5,
      "summary": "An explanation of XanaNode as a layered protocol, not a single viewer, website, repository, or graph visualization.",
      "content": "XanaNode is a layered protocol and working stack for knowledge substrates. It is not the website, not the graph alone, not the Hugo projection, and not a single repository.\n\nAt the bottom, the protocol defines the shared rules: canonical schemas, registries, artifact contracts, validation expectations, and governance conventions. Core reads and writes those protocol records. Workspace manages substrate folders, bundles, and federation intake. Studio and Mobile are authoring tools built on top of that substrate model. Hugo is one projection layer that renders the same underlying substrate as a public static site.\n\nThat layering matters because the knowledge should remain usable even when one projection changes. A substrate can be moved to another tool, shared as a `.substrate` bundle, mounted from a registry target, or rendered through a different projection without rewriting its meaning from scratch.\n\nXanaNode is also an explicit answer to a three-part computing failure: document boundaries collapse knowledge structure, copied excerpts lose durable lineage, and local-first tools without a shared substrate protocol still do not federate cleanly. The protocol tries to solve that by keeping relationships first-class, making fragments addressable, preserving raw assets and provenance, and separating the substrate from any one projection layer.\n\nThe canonical XanaNode substrate is intentionally self-referential. It carries its own schemas, artifacts, sources, branding, project nodes, implementation links, and explanatory trails so the stack can demonstrate the thing it claims to provide.",
      "relationships": []
    },
    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:essay/xananode-as-a-kernel-for-human-thought",
      "title": "XanaNode As A Kernel For Human Thought",
      "type": "essay",
      "subtype": "analysis",
      "importance": 5,
      "summary": "A canonical essay explaining XanaNode as an open substrate layer for knowledge, with stable fragment transclusions that tie the comparison back to the authored source text and the protocol's core claims.",
      "created_by": "xananode.canonical:person/christian-siefen",
      "created_at": "2026-06-22T00:00:00.000Z",
      "content": "XanaNode makes more sense when compared to Linux than when compared to another notes app.\n\nLinux became important because it was not merely a product. It was an open kernel: a shared plumbing layer other people could build on, distribute differently, and keep using even when one interface, company, or distribution changed. Android, servers, embedded devices, and supercomputers all diverged at the projection layer while still relying on the same foundational contract underneath.\n\nXanaNode is trying to do that for knowledge.\n\nThe protocol defines the common contract. Core enforces the substrate shape, fragment model, validation, and export behavior. Workspace manages folders, portable `.substrate` bundles, and federation intake. Studio, Mobile, and Hugo are projections over that same substrate rather than isolated silos pretending to be the knowledge itself.\n\nThat separation is the point. If the substrate outlives a projection, then the knowledge has not been taken hostage by a single product decision. A viewer can fail. A pricing model can change. A company can disappear. The substrate should still open somewhere else with its identities, relationships, provenance, fragments, and raw assets intact.\n\nThis is where the protocol's three recurring failure claims come together. Document boundaries collapse knowledge structure. Copy-and-paste reuse breaks lineage. Local-first storage without federation still leaves knowledge trapped inside tool-specific habits. XanaNode tries to solve all three by making the structure explicit and portable.\n\nThe Linux comparison also clarifies why federation matters. Linux succeeded because many independent actors could share a common base without surrendering ownership of their own layers. XanaNode aims for the same pattern with independently authored substrates that can be mounted, compared, merged, and projected without flattening disagreement or provenance.\n\nThe goal is not one more application. The goal is an open substrate ecosystem for durable human-to-machine knowledge transfer.\n\n## Transcluded anchor passages\n\nThis essay transcludes four addressable fragments from the authored source node `xananode.canonical:source/xananode-kernel-for-human-thought-draft`.\n\n- `xananode.canonical:source/xananode-kernel-for-human-thought-draft@sha256:xananode-kernel-for-human-thought-draft-source-v1#fragment/0001@sha256:xananode-kernel-thought-fragment-0001`\n- `xananode.canonical:source/xananode-kernel-for-human-thought-draft@sha256:xananode-kernel-for-human-thought-draft-source-v1#fragment/0002@sha256:xananode-kernel-thought-fragment-0002`\n- `xananode.canonical:source/xananode-kernel-for-human-thought-draft@sha256:xananode-kernel-for-human-thought-draft-source-v1#fragment/0003@sha256:xananode-kernel-thought-fragment-0003`\n- `xananode.canonical:source/xananode-kernel-for-human-thought-draft@sha256:xananode-kernel-for-human-thought-draft-source-v1#fragment/0004@sha256:xananode-kernel-thought-fragment-0004`\n\nThose tumbler addresses are the proof that the comparison can be preserved as referenceable structure instead of becoming another anonymous copy.",
      "relationships": []
    },
    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:essay/xananode-compared-to-networked-notes-tools",
      "title": "XanaNode compared to networked notes tools",
      "type": "essay",
      "subtype": "comparison",
      "importance": 4,
      "summary": "A comparison between XanaNode and note-linking tools such as Logseq, Obsidian, and Anytype, focusing on protocol-level portability, transclusion, lineage, and federation.",
      "content": "Tools such as Logseq, Obsidian, and Anytype move closer to networked thought than ordinary flat documents do. They help people connect ideas, reuse material, and work locally with less dependence on one central service.\n\nXanaNode is not a rejection of those tools. It is an attempt to define the missing protocol layer beneath them.\n\nLogseq is strong at block-oriented thinking. Obsidian is strong at page-linking and a broad plugin ecosystem. Anytype is strong at object-oriented modeling and local-first ownership. What they do not yet provide in the same way is a shared, renderer-independent substrate contract for independently authored nodes, typed relationships, addressable fragments, portable raw assets, explicit provenance, mergeable federation, and many possible projection layers.\n\nIn other words, those tools can help a person think in a networked way. XanaNode tries to make the knowledge itself portable as a network, even when it leaves the original tool.\n\nThat is the deeper goal behind the comparison to Bush, Engelbart, Nelson, and Douglas Adams's Hyperland demonstration. The point is not just to create nicer linked notes. The point is to preserve the structure of thought, the source path behind it, and the assets that support it in a form that can survive transmission.",
      "relationships": []
    },

      "content": "The substrate and the projection are intentionally separated. In XanaNode, the same substrate can be viewed through Studio, Mobile, Hugo, or a future projection that does not exist yet. The tool is a lens over the data, not the data itself.",

    {

    {

    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:question/how-do-i-use-xananode-myself",
      "title": "How do I use XanaNode myself?",
      "type": "question",
      "subtype": "faq",
      "importance": 5,
      "summary": "Start with a substrate, add nodes and typed relationships, validate it with Core, and project or capture it with tools such as Studio, Mobile, or Hugo.",
      "content": "A user can begin with a small question, claim, source, or trail. Core can initialize a substrate, validate nodes and relationships, generate review suggestions, and build bundles. Mobile can capture notes, files, URLs, and field media into a compliant working substrate. Studio can open a substrate as a local working copy so authors can edit without pretending they own someone else's substrate. Hugo can mount or import validated substrate data for a public website.",
      "relationships": []
    },
    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:question/how-do-sharing-rules-work",
      "title": "How do sharing rules work?",
      "type": "question",
      "subtype": "faq",
      "importance": 5,
      "summary": "Nodes are shareable by default, but a substrate can exclude individual nodes, relationships, trails, or selected groups from shared exports.",
      "content": "XanaNode assumes portability unless an author says otherwise. A private workspace can hold a large substrate while its pack export omits sensitive records. Sharing rules can live on the substrate manifest or on individual nodes, and official tools must respect those rules before publishing, exporting, mounting, or federating records.",
      "relationships": []
    },
    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:question/is-xananode-a-real-working-stack",
      "title": "Is XanaNode a real working stack?",
      "type": "question",
      "subtype": "faq",
      "importance": 5,
      "summary": "Yes. XanaNode has a protocol, Core SDK, Workspace layer, Studio authoring application, Mobile capture companion, and Hugo projection layer that work together.",
      "content": "XanaNode is not only a design idea. The current stack includes protocol schemas and governance rules, a Core SDK for validation and pack building, Workspace for local substrate handling, Studio for desktop authoring, Mobile for capture-first substrate intake, and Hugo for static public projection. The pieces are versioned so a reader can trace what each part does and why it exists.",
      "relationships": []
    },
    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:question/who-made-xananode",
      "title": "Who made XanaNode?",
      "type": "question",
      "subtype": "faq",
      "importance": 5,
      "summary": "XanaNode is authored by Christian Siefen. Built By Bots remains part of the project's historical lineage, but XanaNode.com is the long-term public home of the work.",
      "content": "The canonical project substrate identifies Christian Siefen as the human author of XanaNode. It also preserves historical lineage records, including the temporary Built By Bots bridge period, the current XanaNode.com domain, the public repositories, branding, and support links. That provenance matters because people should be able to trace who authored a substrate, how the public identity evolved, and where project work now lives.",
      "relationships": []
    },
    {
      "id": "xananode.canonical:question/why-does-xananode-exist",
      "title": "Why does XanaNode exist?",
      "type": "question",
      "subtype": "faq",
      "importance": 5,
      "summary": "XanaNode exists to preserve relationships, provenance, disagreement, lineage, and reusable fragments as durable knowledge structure.",
      "content": "A normal website can publish text. XanaNode publishes the relationships behind the text. It is meant for authored knowledge substrates where sources, claims, trails, versions, transclusions, conflicts, and projection layers remain inspectable instead of disappearing into a flat page.",
      "relationships": []
    },

      "content": "{\n  \"id\": \"example.merge:report/federation-example-001\",\n  \"status\": \"review-needed\",\n  \"generated_at\": \"2026-01-01T00:00:00Z\",\n  \"substrates\": [\n    \"example.researcher_a\",\n    \"example.researcher_b\"\n  ],\n  \"compatibility_level\": 4,\n  \"mappings\": [\n    {\n      \"id\": \"example.merge:mapping/knowledge-memory-substrate\",\n      \"source\": \"example.researcher_a:concept/knowledge-substrate\",\n      \"target\": \"example.researcher_b:concept/memory-substrate\",\n      \"type\": \"possible_match\",\n      \"confidence\": 0.78,\n      \"status\": \"review-needed\",\n      \"preserves_local_nodes\": true,\n      \"creates_relationships\": [\n        \"example.researcher_a:rel/knowledge-substrate-possible-match-memory-substrate\",\n        \"example.researcher_b:rel/memory-substrate-possible-match-knowledge-substrate\"\n      ],\n      \"rationale\": \"Both nodes describe substrate-like structures for preserving context and relationships, but one emphasizes knowledge objects while the other emphasizes memory over time.\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"conflicts\": [\n    {\n      \"id\": \"example.merge:conflict/do-not-collapse-concepts\",\n      \"type\": \"identity_ambiguity\",\n      \"nodes\": [\n        \"example.researcher_a:concept/knowledge-substrate\",\n        \"example.researcher_b:concept/memory-substrate\"\n      ],\n      \"resolution\": \"preserve_both\",\n      \"summary\": \"The merge pass records a possible match but does not replace either local concept with a canonical node.\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"warnings\": [\n    \"The two concepts are close but should not be automatically collapsed into a single canonical node.\"\n  ]\n}\n",
      "asset_path": "assets/raw/protocol/examples/federation-example/merge-report.json",
      "content_id": "sha256:5ef3669d327ba167955d42868fee5e3975fb15d7ecb786ea10d20b8cb3ebad50",

        "tool": "@xananode/core"

      "content": "{\n  \"id\": \"example.minimal\",\n  \"name\": \"Minimal XanaNode Substrate\",\n  \"description\": \"A tiny but complete XanaNode substrate showing that every meaningful node is connected through typed relationships, not only by a trail.\",\n  \"version\": \"0.2.0\",\n  \"namespace\": \"example.minimal\",\n  \"repository\": {\n    \"type\": \"git\",\n    \"url\": \"https://github.com/kingc95/xananode.git\",\n    \"default_branch\": \"main\",\n    \"path\": \"examples/minimal-substrate\"\n  },\n  \"federation\": {\n    \"feed\": \"https://example.com/xananode/minimal/feed.json\",\n    \"merge_reports\": \"https://example.com/xananode/minimal/merge-reports/\"\n  },\n  \"trust\": {\n    \"signed\": false,\n    \"trust_scope\": [\n      \"local_substrate\",\n      \"assertion\"\n    ]\n  },\n  \"schema_version\": \"xananode-core@0.5.0\",\n  \"license\": \"CC0-1.0\",\n  \"branding\": {\n    \"brand_name\": \"XanaNode\",\n    \"tagline\": \"Relationships preserve knowledge\",\n    \"description\": \"Canonical XanaNode protocol branding used by protocol examples and downstream project tools.\",\n    \"primary_media\": \"example.minimal:media/xananode-icon\",\n    \"logo_media\": \"example.minimal:media/xananode-icon\",\n    \"icon_media\": \"example.minimal:media/xananode-icon\",\n    \"primary_color\": \"#55D6BE\",\n    \"accent_color\": \"#FF8C00\",\n    \"background_color\": \"#05070A\",\n    \"text_color\": \"#F8FAFC\",\n    \"source_node\": \"example.minimal:media/xananode-icon\",\n    \"rights_status\": \"open-source\",\n    \"license\": \"MIT\",\n    \"updated_at\": \"2026-06-19T00:00:00Z\",\n    \"reason\": \"Protocol branding is modeled as substrate metadata and media so changes to project identity can be preserved as knowledge.\"\n  },\n  \"maintainers\": [\n    {\n      \"name\": \"Example Maintainer\",\n      \"url\": \"https://example.com\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"imports\": [\n    \"xananode:core\"\n  ]\n}\n",

    \"namespace\": \"xananode.protocol\",\n      \"description\": \"A substrate source built directly from the XanaNode Protocol repository, preserving schemas, registries, specs, governance, raw source files, and protocol-defining documents as first-class XanaNode records.\",\n      \"status\": \"canonical\",\n      \"repository\": {\n        \"type\": \"git\",\n        \"url\": Substrate\",\n      \"namespace\": \"xananode.canonical\",\n      \"description\": \"The canonical XanaNode substrate for protocol vocabulary, project identity, governance, software stack records, raw source snapshots, branding, and self-referential examples.\",\n      \"status\": \"canonical\",\n      \"repository\": {\n        \"type\": \"git\",\n        \"url\": \"https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Canonical-Substrate.git\",\n        \"default_branch\": \"main\"\n      },\n      \"recommended_mode\": \"mounted\",\n      \"licenses\": [\"CC-BY-4.0\", \"Apache-2.0\"],\n      \"homepage\": \"https://xananode.com/\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"id\": \"xananode.core\",\n      \"name\": \"XanaNode Core SDK Substrate\",\n      \"namespace\": \"xananode.core\",\n      \"description\": \"A substrate source built directly from the XanaNode Core SDK repository, preserving reference code, schemas, registries, templates, tests, raw project documents, and executable-contract artifacts as first-class XanaNode records.\",\n      \"status\": \"official\",\n      \"repository\": {\n        \"type\": \"git\",\n        \"url\": \"https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Core-SDK.git\",\n        \"default_branch\": \"main\",\n        \"substrate_path\": \"substrate-source\"\n      },\n      \"recommended_mode\": \"mounted\",\n      \"licenses\": [\"Apache-2.0\"],\n      \"homepage\": \"https://xananode.com/\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"id\": \"xananode.workspace\",\n      \"name\": \"XanaNode Workspace Substrate\",\n      \"namespace\": \"xananode.workspace\",\n      \"description\": \"A substrate source built directly from the XanaNode Workspace repository, preserving the local-first workspace engine, CLI surface, templates, tests, raw project documents, and packaged executable artifacts as first-class XanaNode records.\",\n      \"status\": \"official\",\n      \"repository\": {\n        \"type\": \"git\",\n \"mounted\",\n      \"licenses\": [\"Apache-2.0\"],\n      \"homepage\": \"https://xananode.com/\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"id\": \"xananode.studio\",\n      \"name\": \"XanaNode Studio Substrate\",\n main-process logic, scripts, tests, and raw project documents as first-class XanaNode records.\",\n      \"status\": \"official\",\n      \"repository\": {\n        \"type\": \"git\",\n        \"url\": \"https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Studio.git\",\n        \"default_branch\": \"main\",\n        \"substrate_path\": \"substrate-source\"\n      },\n      \"recommended_mode\": \"mounted\",\n      \"licenses\": [\"Apache-2.0\"],\n      \"homepage\": \"https://xananode.com/\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"id\": \"xananode.mobile\",\n      \"name\": \"XanaNode Mobile Substrate\",\n flows, mobile assets, verifier bridge scripts, and raw project documents as first-class XanaNode records.\",\n      \"status\": \"official\",\n      \"repository\": {\n        \"type\": \"git\",\n \"mounted\",\n      \"licenses\": [\"Apache-2.0\"],\n      \"homepage\": \"https://xananode.com/\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"id\": \"xananode.hugo\",\n      \"name\": \"XanaNode Hugo Theme \"url\": \"https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Hugo.git\",\n        \"default_branch\": \"main\",\n        \"substrate_path\": \"substrate-source\"\n      },\n      \"recommended_mode\":       "content": "{\n  \"$schema\": \"https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema\",\n  \"$id\": \"https://xananode.org/registry/federation-targets.schema.json\",\n  \"title\": \"XanaNode Federation Targets Registry\",\n  \"type\": \"object\",\n  \"additionalProperties\": false,\n  \"required\": [\"federation_targets\"],\n  \"properties\": {\n    \"federation_targets\": {\n      \"type\": \"array\",\n      \"items\": {\n        \"type\": \"object\",\n        \"additionalProperties\": false,\n        \"required\": [\"id\", \"name\", \"namespace\", \"repository\"],\n        \"properties\": {\n          \"id\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n          \"name\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n          \"namespace\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n          \"description\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n          \"status\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"enum\": [\"canonical\", \"community\", \"example\", \"experimental\", \"archived\"]\n          },\n          \"repository\": {\n            \"type\": \"object\",\n            \"additionalProperties\": false,\n            \"required\": [\"type\", \"url\", \"default_branch\"],\n            \"properties\": {\n              \"type\": {\n                \"type\": \"string\",\n                \"enum\": [\"git\"]\n              },\n              \"url\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n              \"default_branch\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n              \"substrate_path\": { \"type\": \"string\" }\n            }\n          },\n          \"recommended_mode\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"enum\": [\"mounted\", \"imported\", \"merged\", \"absorbed\"]\n          },\n          \"licenses\": {\n            \"type\": \"array\",\n            \"items\": { \"type\": \"string\" }\n          },\n          \"homepage\": { \"type\": \"string\", \"format\": \"uri\" }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n",
      "asset_path": "assets/raw/protocol/registry/federation-targets.schema.json",

      "content": "{\n  \"implementations\": [\n    {\n      \"name\": \"XanaNode Protocol\",\n      \"type\": \"protocol\",\n      \"status\": \"active\",\n      \"url\": \"https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode\",\n      \"repository\": \"kingc95/XanaNode\",\n      \"protocol_role\": \"schema-and-governance-source\",\n      \"description\": \"Canonical XanaNode protocol repository containing schemas, registries, specs, governance, examples, branding, and proposals.\",\n      \"consumes\": [],\n      \"related_protocol_artifacts\": [\n        \"../schemas/\",\n        \"../registry/\",\n        \"../specs/\",\n        \"../governance/\",\n        \"../examples/minimal-substrate/\"\n      ]\n    },\n    {\n      \"name\": \"XanaNode Core SDK\",\n      \"type\": \"core-sdk\",\n      \"status\": \"active\",\n      \"url\": \"https://github.com/kingc95/XanaNode-Core-SDK\",\n      \"repository\": \"kingc95/XanaNode-Core-SDK\",\n      \"protocol_role\": \"reference-core-engine\",\n      \"description\": \"Renderer-independent parser, validator, graph builder, fragment engine, pack composer, suggestion engine, and exporter for XanaNode substrates.\",\n      \"consumes\": [\n        \"protocol schemas\",\n        \"node files\",\n        \"relationship files\",\n        \"substrate manifests\",\n        \"substrate packs\"\n      ],\n      \"related_protocol_artifacts\": [\n        \"../schemas/substrate-manifest.schema.json\",\n        \"../schemas/substrate-node.schema.json\",\n       "media_type": "document",

      "rights_status": "Apache-2.0",
      "content": "{\n  \"$schema\": \"https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema\",\n  \"$id\": \"https://xananode.org/schemas/author-profile.schema.json\",\n  \"title\": \"Author Profile\",\n  \"type\": \"object\",\n  \"additionalProperties\": false,\n  \"required\": [\"id\", \"name\"],\n  \"properties\": {\n    \"id\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"name\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"orcid\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"github\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"website\": { \"type\": \"string\", \"format\": \"uri\" },\n    \"branding\": {\n      \"$ref\": \"#/$defs/branding\"\n    },\n    \"public_key\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"default_rights\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"default_license\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"default_role\": { \"type\": \"string\" }\n  },\n  \"$defs\": {\n    \"branding\": {\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"description\": \"Portable branding metadata for an author, organization, substrate, pack, or projection. Branding is knowledge provenance, not renderer-only decoration.\",\n      \"additionalProperties\": false,\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"brand_name\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"tagline\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"description\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"primary_media\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"logo_media\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"icon_media\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"primary_color\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"pattern\": \"^#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}$\"\n        },\n        \"accent_color\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"pattern\": \"^#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}$\"\n        },\n        \"background_color\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"pattern\": \"^#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}$\"\n        },\n        \"text_color\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"pattern\": \"^#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}$\"\n        },\n        \"source_node\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"rights_status\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"license\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"updated_at\": { \"type\": \"string\", \"format\": \"date-time\" },\n        \"reason\": { \"type\": \"string\" }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n"
    },

},\n    \"compatibility_level\": {\n      \"type\": \"integer\",\n      \"minimum\": 0,\n      \"maximum\": 5\n    },\n    \"base\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"ours\": { \"type\": \"string\" \"source\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n          \"target\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n          \"type\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n          \"confidence\": {\n            \"type\": \"number\",\n       "content": "{\n  \"$schema\": \"https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema\",\n  \"$id\": \"https://xananode.org/schemas/property-registry.schema.json\",\n  \"title\": \"XanaNode Property Registry\",\n  \"type\": \"object\",\n  \"additionalProperties\": false,\n  \"required\": [\"id\", \"version\", \"property_registry\"],\n  \"properties\": {\n    \"$schema\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"id\": {\n      \"type\": \"string\",\n      \"pattern\": \"^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*(\\\\.[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*)*:[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*$\"\n    },\n    \"version\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"description\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"property_registry\": {\n      \"type\": \"array\",\n      \"items\": {\n        \"type\": \"object\",\n        \"additionalProperties\": false,\n        \"required\": [\"id\", \"label\", \"category\", \"value_schema\"],\n        \"properties\": {\n          \"id\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"pattern\": \"^([a-z][a-z0-9_-]*(\\\\.[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*)*:)?[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*$\"\n          },\n          \"label\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n          \"description\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n       "content": "{\r\n  \"$schema\": \"https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema\",\r\n  \"$id\": \"https://xananode.org/schemas/substrate-manifest.schema.json\",\r\n  \"title\": \"Substrate Manifest\",\r\n  \"type\": \"object\",\r\n  \"additionalProperties\": false,\r\n  \"required\": [\"id\", \"name\", \"version\", \"namespace\", \"repository\"],\n  \"properties\": {\r\n    \"id\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\r\n    \"name\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\r\n    \"version\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\r\n    \"namespace\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"repository\": {\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"additionalProperties\": false,\n      \"required\": [\"type\", \"url\", \"default_branch\"],\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"type\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"enum\": [\"git\"]\n        },\n        \"url\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"default_branch\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"path\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"commit\": { \"type\": \"string\" }\n      }\n    },\n    \"federation\": {\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"additionalProperties\": false,\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"inbox\": { \"type\": \"string\", \"format\": \"uri\" },\n        \"outbox\": { \"type\": \"string\", \"format\": \"uri\" },\n        \"feed\": { \"type\": \"string\", \"format\": \"uri\" },\n        \"merge_reports\": { \"type\": \"string\", \"format\": \"uri\" },\n        \"mapping_endpoint\": { \"type\": \"string\", \"format\": \"uri\" }\n      }\n    },\n    \"trust\": {\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"additionalProperties\": false,\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"signed\": { \"type\": \"boolean\" },\n        \"signature\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"public_key\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"trust_scope\": {\n          \"type\": \"array\",\n          \"items\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"enum\": [\"local_substrate\", \"imported_schema\", \"mapping\", \"assertion\"]\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    },\n    \"description\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"branding\": {\n      \"$ref\": \"#/$defs/branding\"\n    },\n    \"build_metadata\": {\n      \"$ref\": \"#/$defs/build_metadata\"\n    },\n    \"schema_version\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"license\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"sharing\": {\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"description\": \"Substrate-level sharing policy. The default is shareable unless a node, trail, or explicit rule excludes records.\",\n      \"additionalProperties\": false,\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"default_shareable\": {\n          \"type\": \"boolean\",\n          \"description\": \"Whether nodes are shareable by default when they do not declare their own sharing rule.\"\n        },\n        \"excluded_nodes\": {\n          \"type\": \"array\",\n          \"description\": \"Node ids that must not be included in shared exports or mounted public projections.\",\n          \"items\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n          \"uniqueItems\": true\n        },\n        \"excluded_trails\": {\n          \"type\": \"array\",\n          \"description\": \"Trail node ids whose listed trail nodes should be excluded unless a node explicitly opts back in.\",\n          \"items\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n          \"uniqueItems\": true\n        },\n        \"excluded_relationships\": {\n          \"type\": \"array\",\n          \"description\": \"Relationship ids that must not be included in shared exports or mounted public projections.\",\n          \"items\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n          \"uniqueItems\": true\n        },\n        \"rules\": {\n          \"type\": \"array\",\n          \"description\": \"Named sharing rules for selectors a tool understands, such as type, subtype, tag, path, trail, or namespace.\",\n          \"items\": {\n            \"type\": \"object\",\n            \"additionalProperties\": false,\n            \"required\": [\"selector\", \"shareable\"],\n            \"properties\": {\n              \"selector\": {\n                \"type\": \"object\",\n                \"additionalProperties\": true,\n                \"description\": \"Selector interpreted by tools, commonly {type}, {subtype}, {tag}, {trail}, {path}, or {namespace}.\"\n              },\n              \"shareable\": { \"type\": \"boolean\" },\n              \"scope\": {\n                \"type\": \"string\",\n                \"enum\": [\"public\", \"unlisted\", \"trusted\", \"private\"]\n              },\n              \"reason\": { \"type\": \"string\" }\n            }\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    },\n    \"maintainers\": {\n      \"type\": \"array\",\n      \"items\": {\n        \"type\": \"object\",\n        \"additionalProperties\": false,\n        \"required\": [\"name\"],\n        \"properties\": {\n          \"name\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n          \"url\": { \"type\": \"string\", \"format\": \"uri\" }\n        }\n      }\n    },\n    \"imports\": {\n      \"type\": \"array\",\n      \"items\": {\n        \"oneOf\": [\n          { \"type\": \"string\" },\n          {\n            \"type\": \"object\",\n            \"additionalProperties\": false,\n            \"required\": [\"id\", \"source\", \"mode\"],\n            \"properties\": {\n              \"id\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n              \"source\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n              \"mode\": {\n                \"type\": \"string\",\n                \"enum\": [\"mounted\", \"imported\", \"merged\", \"absorbed\"]\n              },\n              \"version\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n              \"description\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n              \"required\": { \"type\": \"boolean\" },\n              \"repository\": {\n                \"type\": \"object\",\n                \"additionalProperties\": false,\n                \"properties\": {\n                  \"type\": {\n                    \"type\": \"string\",\n                    \"enum\": [\"git\"]\n                  },\n                  \"url\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n                  \"branch\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n                  \"commit\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n                  \"archive\": { \"type\": \"string\" }\n                }\n              },\n              \"archive\": {\n                \"type\": \"object\",\n                \"additionalProperties\": false,\n                \"properties\": {\n                  \"media_type\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n                  \"file\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n                  \"sha256\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n                  \"created_at\": { \"type\": \"string\", \"format\": \"date-time\" }\n                }\n              },\n              \"namespace_mappings\": {\n                \"type\": \"array\",\n                \"items\": {\n                  \"type\": \"object\",\n                  \"additionalProperties\": false,\n                  \"required\": [\"from\", \"to\", \"scope\"],\n                  \"properties\": {\n                    \"from\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n                    \"to\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n                    \"scope\": {\n                      \"type\": \"string\",\n                      \"enum\": [\"relationships\", \"all\"]\n                    },\n                    \"reason\": { \"type\": \"string\" }\n                  }\n                }\n              },\n              \"trust\": {\n                \"type\": \"object\",\n                \"additionalProperties\": false,\n                \"properties\": {\n                  \"signed\": { \"type\": \"boolean\" },\n                  \"signature\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n                  \"public_key\": { \"type\": \"string\" }\n                }\n              }\n            }\n          }\n        ]\n      }\n    },\n    \"pack\": {\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"description\": \"Metadata used when this manifest describes a portable substrate pack rather than only a live authored substrate.\",\n      \"additionalProperties\": false,\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"mode\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"enum\": [\"mounted\", \"imported\", \"merged\", \"absorbed\"]\n        },\n        \"built_by\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"built_at\": { \"type\": \"string\", \"format\": \"date-time\" },\n        \"build_metadata\": {\n          \"$ref\": \"#/$defs/build_metadata\"\n        },\n        \"portable\": { \"type\": \"boolean\" },\n        \"archive_name\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"archive_media_type\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"source_manifests\": {\n          \"type\": \"array\",\n          \"items\": {\n            \"type\": \"object\",\n            \"additionalProperties\": true,\n            \"properties\": {\n              \"id\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n              \"name\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n              \"namespace\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n              \"version\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n              \"repository\": { \"type\": \"object\" }\n            }\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    },\n    \"extensions\": {\n      \"type\": \"array\",\n      \"items\": { \"type\": \"string\" }\n    }\n  },\n  \"$defs\": {\n    \"branding\": {\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"description\": \"Portable branding metadata for a substrate or pack. Branding assets should be represented as media nodes and referenced here by node id.\",\n      \"additionalProperties\": false,\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"brand_name\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"tagline\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"description\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"primary_media\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"logo_media\": { \"type\": \"accent_color\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"pattern\": \"^#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}$\"\n        },\n        \"background_color\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"pattern\": \"^#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}$\"\n        },\n        \"text_color\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"pattern\": \"^#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}$\"\n        },\n        \"source_node\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"rights_status\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"license\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"updated_at\": { \"type\": \"string\", \"format\": \"date-time\" },\n        \"reason\": { \"type\": \"string\" }\n      }\n    },\n    \"build_metadata\": {\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"description\": \"Build and runtime provenance for generated substrates, packs, renderer output, and software-created artifacts.\",\n      \"additionalProperties\": false,\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"version\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"git_commit\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"git_branch\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"repository\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"built_at\": { \"type\": \"string\", \"format\": \"date-time\" },\n        \"compiled_at\": { \"type\": \"string\", \"format\": \"date-time\" },\n        \"generated_at\": { \"type\": \"string\", \"format\": \"date-time\" },\n        \"built_by\": { \"type\":  }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n"

      "content": "{\r\n  \"$schema\": \"https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema\",\r\n  \"$id\": \"https://xananode.org/schemas/substrate-node.schema.json\",\r\n  \"title\": \"Substrate Node\",\r\n  \"type\": \"object\",\r\n  \"additionalProperties\": true,\r\n  \"required\": [\"id\", \"title\", \"type\", \"importance\", \"summary\", \"relationships\"],\r\n  \"properties\": {\r\n    \"id\": {\r\n      \"type\": \"string\",\r\n      \"pattern\": \"^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*(\\\\.[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*)*:[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*/[^\\\\s#]+$\"\r\n    },\r\n    \"title\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\r\n    \"type\": {\n      \"type\": \"string\",\n      \"pattern\": \"^([a-z][a-z0-9_-]*(\\\\.[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*)*:)?[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*$\"\n    },\n    \"subtype\": {\n      \"type\": \"string\",\n      \"description\": \"A narrower kind within the primary node type, such as git_repository, official_site, documentation, support_page, screenshot, logo, or web_snapshot.\",\n      \"pattern\": \"^([a-z][a-z0-9_-]*(\\\\.[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*)*:)?[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*$\"\n    },\n    \"subtypes\": {\n      \"type\": \"array\",\n      \"description\": \"Additional narrower kinds within the primary node type. Use when one node needs more than one subtype without changing its primary type.\",\n      \"items\": {\n        \"type\": \"string\",\n        \"pattern\": \"^([a-z][a-z0-9_-]*(\\\\.[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*)*:)?[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*$\"\n      },\n      \"uniqueItems\": true\n    },\n    \"facets\": {\n      \"type\": \"array\",\n      \"description\": \"Secondary node roles used when one knowledge object participates in multiple modeling categories without duplicating its identity.\",\n      \"items\": {\n        \"type\": \"string\",\n        \"pattern\": \"^([a-z][a-z0-9_-]*(\\\\.[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*)*:)?[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*$\"\n      },\n      \"uniqueItems\": true\n    },\n    \"status\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\r\n    \"importance\": {\r\n      \"type\": \"integer\",\r\n      \"minimum\": 1,\r\n      \"maximum\": 5\r\n    },\r\n    \"summary\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\r\n    \"created_by\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\r\n    \"created_at\": { \"type\": \"string\", \"format\": \"date-time\" },\r\n    \"updated_at\": { \"type\": \"string\", \"format\": \"date-time\" },\r\n    \"source_url\": { \"type\": \"string\", \"format\": \"uri\" },\n    \"rights_status\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"sharing\": {\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"description\": \"Node-level sharing policy. Nodes are shareable by default unless this object or a substrate-level rule says otherwise.\",\n      \"additionalProperties\": false,\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"shareable\": {\n          \"type\": \"boolean\",\n          \"description\": \"Whether this node may be included in exported, published, federated, or shared packs. Defaults to true.\"\n        },\n        \"scope\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"description\": \"The intended sharing scope for tools that support private workspaces or limited review.\",\n          \"enum\": [\"public\", \"unlisted\", \"trusted\", \"private\"]\n        },\n        \"reason\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"review_status\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"enum\": [\"allowed\", \"blocked\", \"needs_review\"]\n        }\n      }\n    },\n    \"asset_path\": {\n      \"type\": \"string\",\n      \"description\": \"Portable relative path to a local asset file inside the substrate or pack, usually used by media nodes.\"\n    },\n    \"asset_role\": {\n      \"type\": \"string\",\n      \"description\": \"The role this asset plays for the node, such as primary_media, source_snapshot, thumbnail, transcript, attachment, or archival_copy.\"\n    },\n    \"media_type\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"mime_type\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"alt\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"caption\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"source_snapshot\": {\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"description\": \"Metadata for a captured representation of an external source page.\",\n      \"additionalProperties\": false,\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"captured_at\": { \"type\": \"string\", \"format\": \"date-time\" },\n        \"source_url\": { \"type\": \"string\", \"format\": \"uri\" },\n        \"method\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"enum\": [\"opengraph\", \"screenshot\", \"readability\", \"manual\", \"archive\"]\n        },\n        \"content_id\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"rights_status\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"tool\": { \"type\": \"string\" }\n      }\n    },\n    \"confidence\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"uncertainty_level\": {\n      \"type\": \"string\",\n      \"description\": \"A canonical epistemic uncertainty level for questions, hypotheses, claims, knowledge gaps, responses, or first-class relationships.\",\n      \"enum\": [\"unknown\", \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\", \"unresolved\"]\n    },\n    \"review_status\": {\n      \"type\": \"string\",\n      \"description\": \"Workflow review state for a node or relationship being authored, validated, merged, or deprecated.\",\n      \"enum\": [\"draft\", \"needs_review\", \"under_review\", \"approved\", \"rejected\", \"deprecated\"]\n    },\n    \"research_priority\": {\n      \"type\": \"integer\",\n      \"description\": \"Priority signal for inquiry, problems, and knowledge gaps.\",\n      \"minimum\": 1,\n      \"maximum\": 5\n    },\n    \"evidence_strength\": {\n      \"type\": \"string\",\n      \"description\": \"Compact assessment of the evidence available for a claim, hypothesis, response, or relationship.\",\n      \"enum\": [\"none\", \"weak\", \"moderate\", \"strong\", \"decisive\", \"contested\"]\n    },\n    \"communication_model\": {\n      \"type\": \"string\",\n      \"description\": \"Participant topology for a first-class communication.\",\n      \"enum\": [\"one_to_one\", \"one_to_many\", \"many_to_one\", \"many_to_many\"]\n    },\n    \"communication_pattern\": {\n      \"type\": \"string\",\n      \"description\": \"Semantic communication pattern beyond participant count.\",\n      \"enum\": [\n        \"dialogue\",\n        \"broadcast\",\n        \"collection\",\n        \"coordination\",\n        \"negotiation\",\n        \"debate\",\n        \"consensus\",\n        \"instruction\",\n        \"query_response\",\n        \"publication\"\n      ]\n    },\n    \"protocol_id\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"content_id\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"version_id\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"software_version\": {\n      \"type\": \"string\",\n      \"description\": \"Semantic or package version of a software project, component, renderer, SDK, or application.\"\n    },\n    \"build_metadata\": {\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"description\": \"Build and runtime provenance for software nodes, generated packs, and projection artifacts.\",\n      \"additionalProperties\": false,\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"version\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"git_commit\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"git_branch\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"repository\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"built_at\": { \"type\": \"string\", \"format\": \"date-time\" },\n        \"compiled_at\": { \"type\": \"string\", \"format\": \"date-time\" },\n        \"generated_at\": { \"type\": \"string\", \"format\": \"date-time\" },\n        \"built_by\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"runtime\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"platform\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"dependencies\": {\n          \"type\": \"array\",\n          \"items\": {\n            \"type\": \"object\",\n            \"additionalProperties\": false,\n            \"required\": [\"name\"],\n            \"properties\": {\n              \"name\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n              \"version\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n              \"repository\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n              \"relationship\": { \"type\": \"string\" }\n            }\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    },\n    \"source_content_id\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"source_version_id\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"signature\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"source_node\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n    \"fragment_id\": {\r\n      \"type\": \"string\",\r\n      \"pattern\": \"^[A-Za-z0-9._-]+$\"\r\n    },\r\n    \"tumbler\": {\n      \"type\": \"string\",\n      \"pattern\": \"^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*(\\\\.[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*)*:[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*/[^\\\\s#@]+(@[^\\\\s#]+)?(#fragment/[A-Za-z0-9._-]+(@[^\\\\s#]+)?)?$\"\n    },\r\n    \"selector\": {\r\n      \"type\": \"object\",\r\n      \"additionalProperties\": true,\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"type\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"enum\": [\n            \"semantic-anchor\",\n            \"TextQuoteSelector\",\n            \"TextPositionSelector\",\n            \"FragmentSelector\",\n            \"CssSelector\",\n            \"XPathSelector\",\n            \"RangeSelector\"\n          ]\n        },\n        \"value\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"exact\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"prefix\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"suffix\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\n        \"start\": { \"type\": \"integer\" },\n        \"end\": { \"type\": \"integer\" }\n      }\n    },\n    \"relationships\": {\n      \"type\": \"array\",\r\n      \"items\": {\r\n        \"type\": \"object\",\r\n        \"additionalProperties\": false,\r\n        \"required\": [\"id\", \"type\", \"direction\"],\r\n        \"properties\": {\r\n          \"id\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\r\n          \"type\": {\r\n            \"type\": \"string\",\r\n            \"pattern\": \"^([a-z][a-z0-9_-]*(\\\\.[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*)*:)?[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*$\"\r\n          },\r\n          \"source\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\r\n          \"target\": { \"type\": \"string\" },\r\n          \"direction\": {\r\n            \"type\": \"string\",\r\n            \"enum\": [\"incoming\", \"outgoing\"]\r\n          },\r\n          \"external\": { \"type\": \"boolean\" },\r\n          \"target_substrate\": { \"type\": \"string\" }\r\n        }\r\n      }\n    }\n  },\n  \"allOf\": [\n    {\n      \"if\": {\n        \"properties\": {\n          \"type\": { \"const\": \"fragment\" }\n        },\n        \"required\": [\"type\"]\n      },\n      \"then\": {\n        \"required\": [\n          \"source_node\",\n          \"source_version_id\",\n          \"source_content_id\",\n          \"fragment_id\",\n          \"tumbler\",\n          \"selector\",\n          \"content_id\",\n          \"version_id\"\n        ]\n      }\n    }\n  ]\n}\n"

      "content": "{\n  \"version\": \"0.3.0\",\n  \"node_types\": [\n    {\n      \"type\": \"person\",\n      \"label\": \"Person\",\n      \"color\": {\n        \"bg\": \"#8bd3ff\",\n        \"fg\": \"#071827\",\n        \"outline\": \"#d8f1ff\"\n      },\n      \"purpose\": \"A human actor who created, influenced, witnessed, argued, documented, or participated in something.\",\n      \"allowed_subtypes\": [\n        \"writer\",\n        \"researcher\",\n        \"engineer\",\n        \"critic\",\n        \"artist\",\n        \"founder\",\n        \"scientist\",\n        \"programmer\"\n      ],\n      \"required_frontmatter\": [\n        \"title\",\n        \"type\",\n        \"importance\",\n        \"summary\",\n        \"relationships\"\n      ],\n      \"recommended_frontmatter\": [\n        \"roles\",\n        \"primary_media\",\n        \"people\",\n        \"concepts\",\n        \"sources\",\n        \"projects\",\n        \"events\"\n      ],\n      \"namespace\": \"xananode\",\n      \"id\": \"xananode:person\",\n      \"core\": true,\n      \"extends\": null,\n      \"projection\": {\n        \"icon\": \"person\",\n        \"icon_label\": \"P\",\n        \"asset_path\": \"assets/projection/node-types/person.svg\",\n        \"subtype_asset_path_template\": \"assets/projection/node-subtypes/person/{subtype}.svg\"\n      }\n    },\n    {\n      \"type\": \"concept\",\n      \"label\": \"Concept\",\n      \"color\": {\n        \"bg\": \"#91f2a6\",\n        \"fg\": \"#062010\",\n        \"outline\": \"#dcffe4\"\n      },\n      \"purpose\": \"An idea, pattern, theory, failure mode, or explanatory structure.       "content": "{\n  \"$schema\": \"https://xananode.org/schemas/property-registry.schema.json\",\n  \"id\": \"xananode.core:property-registry\",\n  \"version\": \"0.1.0\",\n  \"description\": \"Canonical common properties for open-ended substrate node adjectives.\",\n  \"property_registry\": [\n    {\n      \"id\": \"birth_date\",\n      \"label\": \"Birth date\",\n      \"description\": \"Calendar date on which a person, organization, project, or entity began.\",\n      \"category\": \"time\",\n      \"aliases\": [\n        \"born\",\n        \"date_of_birth\",\n        \"founded_date\"\n      ],\n      \"applies_to\": [\n        \"person\",\n        \"organization\",\n        \"project\"\n      ],\n      \"unit_system\": \"ISO-8601\",\n      \"value_schema\": {\n        \"type\": \"string\",\n        \"format\": \"date\"\n      },\n      \"examples\": [\n        \"1955-02-24\"\n      ]\n    },\n    {\n      \"id\": \"geo_coordinates\",\n      \"label\": \"Geographic coordinates\",\n      \"description\": \"WGS84 latitude and longitude coordinates for a place or geographically located entity.\",\n      \"category\": \"space\",\n      \"aliases\": [\n        \"coordinates\",\n        \"lat_lon\",\n        \"location\"\n      ],\n      \"applies_to\": [\n        \"place\",\n        \"event\",\n        \"organization\",\n        \"source\",\n        \"media\"\n      ],\n      \"unit_system\": \"WGS84\",\n      \"value_schema\": {\n        \"type\": \"object\",\n        \"additionalProperties\": false,\n        \"required\": [\n          \"latitude\",\n          \"longitude\"\n        ],\n        \"properties\": {\n          \"latitude\": {\n            \"type\": \"number\",\n            \"minimum\": -90,\n            \"maximum\": 90\n          },\n          \"longitude\": {\n            \"type\": \"number\",\n            \"minimum\": -180,\n            \"maximum\": 180\n          },\n          \"altitude_m\": {\n            \"type\": \"number\"\n          },\n          \"crs\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"const\": \"WGS84\"\n          }\n        }\n      },\n      \"examples\": [\n        {\n          \"latitude\": 37.33182,\n          \"longitude\": -122.03118,\n          \"crs\": \"WGS84\"\n        }\n      ]\n    },\n    {\n      \"id\": \"currency_value\",\n      \"label\": \"Currency value\",\n      \"description\": \"A monetary amount with an ISO 4217 currency code.\",\n      \"category\": \"money\",\n      \"aliases\": [\n        \"price\",\n        \"cost\",\n        \"market_value\",\n        \"amount_money\"\n      ],\n      \"applies_to\": [\n        \"claim\",\n        \"observation\",\n        \"source\",\n        \"project\"\n      ],\n      \"unit_system\": \"ISO-4217\",\n      \"value_schema\": {\n        \"type\": \"object\",\n        \"additionalProperties\": false,\n        \"required\": [\n          \"amount\",\n          \"currency\"\n        ],\n        \"properties\": {\n          \"amount\": {\n            \"type\": \"number\"\n          },\n          \"currency\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"pattern\": \"^[A-Z]{3}$\"\n          },\n          \"as_of\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"format\": \"date-time\"\n          }\n        }\n      },\n      \"examples\": [\n        {\n          \"amount\": 199.99,\n          \"currency\": \"USD\",\n          \"as_of\": \"2026-01-01T00:00:00Z\"\n        }\n      ]\n    },\n    {\n      \"id\": \"measurement_si\",\n      \"label\": \"SI measurement\",\n      \"description\": \"A quantified physical or conceptual measurement expressed in SI-compatible units.\",\n      \"category\": \"quantity\",\n      \"aliases\": [\n        \"measurement\",\n        \"quantity\",\n        \"metric_value\"\n      ],\n      \"unit_system\": \"SI\",\n      \"value_schema\": {\n        \"type\": \"object\",\n        \"additionalProperties\": false,\n        \"required\": [\n          \"value\",\n          \"unit\"\n        ],\n        \"properties\": {\n          \"value\": {\n            \"type\": \"number\"\n          },\n          \"unit\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\"\n          },\n          \"quantity_kind\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\"\n          },\n          \"uncertainty\": {\n            \"type\": \"number\"\n          }\n        }\n      },\n      \"examples\": [\n        {\n          \"value\": 1.82,\n          \"unit\": \"m\",\n          \"quantity_kind\": \"height\"\n        }\n      ]\n    },\n    {\n      \"id\": \"external_identifier\",\n      \"label\": \"External identifier\",\n      \"description\": \"A typed identifier from an external authority or registry.\",\n      \"category\": \"reference\",\n      \"aliases\": [\n        \"wikidata_id\",\n        \"doi\",\n        \"orcid\",\n        \"authority_id\"\n      ],\n      \"unit_system\": \"none\",\n      \"value_schema\": {\n        \"type\": \"object\",\n        \"additionalProperties\": false,\n        \"required\": [\n          \"authority\",\n          \"value\"\n        ],\n        \"properties\": {\n          \"authority\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\"\n          },\n          \"value\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\"\n          },\n          \"url\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"format\": \"uri\"\n          }\n        }\n      },\n      \"examples\": [\n        {\n          \"authority\": \"wikidata\",\n          \"value\": \"Q19837\",\n          \"url\": \"https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q19837\"\n        }\n      ]\n    },\n    {\n      \"id\": \"uncertainty_level\",\n      \"label\": \"Uncertainty level\",\n      \"category\": \"epistemology\",\n      \"description\": \"A normalized level describing how uncertain a node, relationship, answer, or interpretation remains.\",\n      \"aliases\": [\n        \"certainty_level\",\n        \"unknown_level\",\n        \"confidence_gap\"\n      ],\n      \"applies_to\": [\n        \"claim\",\n        \"question\",\n        \"hypothesis\",\n        \"knowledge_gap\",\n        \"relationship\",\n        \"response\"\n      ],\n      \"unit_system\": \"none\",\n      \"value_schema\": {\n        \"type\": \"string\",\n        \"enum\": [\n          \"unknown\",\n          \"low\",\n          \"medium\",\n          \"high\",\n          \"unresolved\"\n        ]\n      },\n      \"examples\": [\n        \"high\"\n      ]\n    },\n    {\n      \"id\": \"review_status\",\n      \"label\": \"Review status\",\n      \"category\": \"workflow\",\n      \"description\": \"The current review state for a node, relationship, pack, claim, or suggested merge.\",\n      \"aliases\": [\n        \"validation_status\",\n        \"curation_status\"\n      ],\n      \"applies_to\": [\n        \"claim\",\n        \"question\",\n        \"hypothesis\",\n        \"knowledge_gap\",\n        \"relationship\",\n        \"source\"\n      ],\n      \"unit_system\": \"none\",\n      \"value_schema\": {\n        \"type\": \"string\",\n \"examples\": [\n        \"needs_review\"\n      ]\n    },\n    {\n      \"id\": \"research_priority\",\n      \"label\": \"Research priority\",\n      \"category\": \"attention\",\n      \"description\": \"A priority signal for deciding which inquiry, problem, or knowledge gap deserves attention first.\",\n      \"aliases\": [\n        \"priority\",\n        \"attention_priority\"\n \"value_schema\": {\n        \"type\": \"integer\",\n        \"minimum\": 1,\n        \"maximum\": 5\n      },\n      \"examples\": [\n        5\n      ]\n    },\n    {\n      \"id\": \"evidence_strength\",\n      \"label\": \"Evidence strength\",\n      \"category\": \"epistemology\",\n      \"description\": \"A compact assessment of the strength of evidence available for a claim, hypothesis, answer, or relationship.\",\n      \"aliases\": [\n        \"support_strength\",\n        \"evidence_quality\"\n      ],\n      \"applies_to\": [\n        \"claim\",\n        \"hypothesis\",\n        \"response\",\n        \"relationship\"\n      ],\n      \"unit_system\": \"none\",\n      \"value_schema\": {\n        \"type\": \"string\",\n        \"enum\": [\n          \"none\",\n          \"weak\",\n          \"moderate\",\n          \"strong\",\n          \"decisive\",\n          \"contested\"\n        ]\n      },\n      \"examples\": [\n        \"moderate\"\n      ]\n    },\n    {\n      \"id\": \"communication_model\",\n      \"label\": \"Communication model\",\n      \"category\": \"communication\",\n      \"description\": \"The participant topology of a communication.\",\n      \"aliases\": [\n        \"communication_topology\",\n        \"topology\"\n      ],\n      \"applies_to\": [\n        \"communication\",\n        \"event\",\n        \"publication\",\n        \"source\"\n      ],\n      \"unit_system\": \"none\",\n      \"value_schema\": {\n        \"type\": \"string\",\n        \"enum\": [\n          \"one_to_one\",\n          \"one_to_many\",\n          \"many_to_one\",\n          \"many_to_many\"\n        ]\n      },\n      \"examples\": [\n        \"many_to_many\"\n      ]\n    },\n    {\n      \"id\": \"communication_pattern\",\n      \"label\": \"Communication pattern\",\n      \"category\": \"communication\",\n      \"description\": \"The semantic pattern of a communication beyond participant count.\",\n      \"aliases\": [\n        \"interaction_pattern\",\n        \"discourse_pattern\"\n      ],\n      \"applies_to\": [\n        \"communication\",\n        \"event\",\n        \"publication\",\n        \"source\"\n      ],\n      \"unit_system\": \"none\",\n      \"value_schema\": {\n        \"type\": \"string\",\n        \"enum\": [\n          \"dialogue\",\n          \"broadcast\",\n          \"collection\",\n          \"coordination\",\n          \"negotiation\",\n          \"debate\",\n          \"consensus\",\n          \"instruction\",\n          \"query_response\",\n          \"publication\"\n        ]\n      },\n      \"examples\": [\n        \"query_response\"\n      ]\n    }\n  ]\n}\n",
